operation.command: find / -maxdepth 3 -type f -perm /o+w -exec chmod o-w {} \; 2>/dev/null; echo 'Permission optimization completed'

risk.reason: LLM assessed: This command modifies permissions of world-writable files across the entire filesystem (maxdepth 3), which is a high-risk operation that can break system functionality. It uses find with exec to change permissions, affecting potentially critical system files.
